steps: Vote to dissolve according to your operating agreement or by majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
Notify all known creditors in writing
File final tax returns with the SC Department of Revenue
File Articles of Termination (Form F0108) with the Secretary of State ($10 fee)
Cancel any business licenses, permits, and trade names
llc.notes: South Carolina uses 'Articles of Termination' rather than 'Articles of Dissolution' for LLCs per SC Code 33-44-805. Tax clearance is not required for voluntary dissolution. Online filing is available through the Business Entities Online system.

steps: Board of directors adopts a resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote per SC Code 33-14-102
Notify all known creditors and claimants in writing
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final tax returns with the SC Department of Revenue
File Articles of Dissolution (Form F0049) with the Secretary of State ($10 fee)
Cancel all business licenses and permits
corporation.notes: Tax clearance is not required for voluntary dissolution. However, if the corporation was administratively dissolved and is seeking reinstatement, a Tax Compliance Certificate from the Department of Revenue is required ($60 fee, Form C-268).

tax.notes: Tax clearance is not required for voluntary dissolution. However, you must file all final tax returns for income, sales, and withholding taxes. If administratively dissolved, a Tax Compliance Certificate (Form C-268, $60 fee) from the Department of Revenue is required for reinstatement. Use the SC Business One Stop (scbos.sc.gov) to close business tax registrations.

requirements.employeeNotification: Must comply with federal WARN Act for layoffs of 100+ employees. Provide final paychecks within 48 hours of termination.
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify all known creditors and claimants in writing with a deadline of at least 120 days to submit claims per SC Code 33-14-106
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete and all affairs are wound up
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Entity continues to exist for purposes of winding up after dissolution is filed per SC Code 33-14-105
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require member vote per operating agreement per SC Code 33-44-801. Corporations require board resolution and shareholder majority approval per SC Code 33-14-102.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
